description: Catechism of the Catholic Church - Key Paragraphs for Daily Reflections

prayer.text: Prayer is the raising of one's mind and heart to God or the requesting of good things from God.
prayer.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

prayer.text: Jesus prays and teaches us to pray. His prayer shows us how to pray to our Father.
prayer.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

prayer.text: Pray at all times with thanksgiving. Rejoice always, pray constantly, give thanks in all circumstances.
prayer.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

prayer.text: Blessing expresses the basic movement of Christian prayer: it is an encounter between God and man.
prayer.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

prayer.text: When we pray for the needs of others, we intercede. Abraham, Moses, and the prophets interceded for the people.
prayer.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

faith.text: By faith, man completely submits his intellect and will to God. With his whole being man gives his assent to God who reveals.
faith.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

faith.text: Faith is necessary for salvation. The Lord himself affirms: He who believes and is baptized will be saved.
faith.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

faith.text: Faith is a personal act and also ecclesial. We believe because the Church believes.
faith.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

faith.text: Faith is the theological virtue by which we believe in God and believe all that he has said and revealed to us.
faith.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

love.text: Charity is the theological virtue by which we love God above all things for his own sake, and our neighbor as ourselves for the love of God.
love.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

love.text: The fruits of charity are joy, peace, and mercy. Charity edifies and bears much fruit.
love.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

love.text: The fruits of the Spirit are perfections that the Holy Spirit forms in us as the first fruits of eternal glory.
love.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

love.text: The fourth commandment illuminates relationships in society. It speaks of our duties to family and community.
love.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

hope.text: Hope is the theological virtue by which we desire the kingdom of heaven and eternal life as our happiness.
hope.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

hope.text: Christian hope unfolds from the beginning of Jesus' preaching in the proclamation of the beatitudes.
hope.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

hope.text: We can therefore hope in the glory of heaven promised by God to those who love him and do his will.
hope.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

suffering.text: Illness can lead to anguish and self-absorption, sometimes even to despair and revolt against God.
suffering.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

suffering.text: Union with the passion of Christ. By the grace of this sacrament the sick person receives the strength and gift of uniting himself more closely to Christ's Passion.
suffering.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

suffering.text: The way of perfection passes by way of the Cross. There is no holiness without renunciation and spiritual battle.
suffering.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

suffering.text: God permits evil in order to draw forth some greater good. Out of the greatest moral evil, God brought the greatest good: the redemption.
suffering.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

forgiveness.text: This 'as' is not unique in Jesus' teaching. Our prayer is answered only if we ourselves have forgiven first.
forgiveness.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

forgiveness.text: Christian prayer extends to the forgiveness of enemies, transfiguring the disciple by configuring him to his Master.
forgiveness.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

forgiveness.text: There is no offense, however serious, that the Church cannot forgive. There is no one so wicked that Christ does not offer salvation.
forgiveness.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

forgiveness.text: This mercy is not merely a word. It is the love that goes beyond measure, the love that forgives.
forgiveness.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

eucharist.text: The Eucharist is the source and summit of the Christian life. All other sacraments are oriented to the Eucharist.
eucharist.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

eucharist.text: In the most blessed sacrament of the Eucharist the body and blood, together with the soul and divinity, of our Lord Jesus Christ is truly, really, and substantially contained.
eucharist.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

eucharist.text: By the consecration the transubstantiation of the bread and wine into the Body and Blood of Christ is brought about.
eucharist.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

mary.text: The Virgin Mary is acknowledged and honored as being truly the Mother of God and of the Redeemer.
mary.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

mary.text: Mary's role in the Church is inseparable from her union with Christ and flows directly from it.
mary.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

mary.text: In prayer we unite ourselves to the heart of Mary, as she pondered these things in her heart.
mary.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

mary.text: Called in the Gospels 'the Mother of Jesus', Mary is acclaimed by Elizabeth as 'the mother of my Lord'.
mary.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

saints.text: By canonizing some of the faithful, the Church proclaims that they practiced heroic virtue and lived in fidelity to God's grace.
saints.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

saints.text: Communion with the saints joins us to Christ, from whom as from its fountain and head issues all grace and all life.
saints.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

saints.text: The intercession of the saints. Being more closely united to Christ, those who dwell in heaven fix the whole Church more firmly in holiness.
saints.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

scripture.text: The Church has always venerated the divine Scriptures just as she venerates the body of the Lord.
scripture.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

scripture.text: In Sacred Scripture the Church finds constant nourishment and strength. She receives with joy the bread of life.
scripture.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

scripture.text: And such is the force and power of the Word of God that it can serve the Church as her support and vigor.
scripture.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

scripture.text: Through all the words of Sacred Scripture, God speaks only one single Word, his one Utterance in whom he expresses himself fully.
scripture.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

church.text: The Church is the People of God. God does not belong exclusively to any one people.
church.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

church.text: The Church was prepared in the Old Covenant and instituted by Christ through his death and resurrection.
church.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

church.text: This is the sole Church of Christ, which in the Creed we profess to be one, holy, catholic and apostolic.
church.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

mercy.text: The Gospel is the revelation in Jesus Christ of God's mercy to sinners.
mercy.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

mercy.text: This mercy is not merely a word. It is the love that goes beyond measure.
mercy.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

mercy.text: God's almighty power is in no way arbitrary. It is the power of a Father who loves his children.
mercy.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

mercy.text: If we refuse to forgive, our prayer cannot be heard. God's mercy cannot penetrate our closed heart.
mercy.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

grace.text: Grace is favor, the free and undeserved help that God gives us to respond to his call.
grace.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

grace.text: Sanctifying grace is a habitual gift, a stable and supernatural disposition that perfects the soul itself.
grace.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

grace.text: Sanctifying grace makes us pleasing to God and enables us to live as his children.
grace.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

peace.text: Respect for and development of human life require peace. Peace is not merely the absence of war.
peace.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

peace.text: Peace is the work of justice and the effect of charity. It requires respect for human dignity.
peace.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

peace.text: Love of country and responsibility for the common good are noble sentiments that promote peace.
peace.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

family.text: The Christian family is a communion of persons, a sign and image of the communion of the Father and the Son.
family.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

family.text: The Christian family constitutes a specific revelation and realization of ecclesial communion.
family.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

family.text: Parents have the first responsibility for the education of their children in the faith.
family.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

default.text: God, infinitely perfect and blessed in himself, created man out of sheer goodness to share in his blessed life.
default.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

default.text: The desire for God is written in the human heart, because man is created by God and for God.
default.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

default.text: Prayer is the raising of one's mind and heart to God.
default.format: Text reference only - paragraph number is universal citation

notes: CCC paragraph numbers are universal citations. Readers can look up in any CCC source (print, online, app).
alternative: Consider text-only CCC references (e.g., "CCC 2842") without hyperlinks for maximum reliability
rationale: Many Catholic publishers use text-only CCC references for reliability. Paragraph numbers work across all editions and platforms.
